# Cross-Dataset Synthesis -- generated by NB7

_Compliance threshold: |DPD| <= 0.1_


## Headline comparison

| Dataset | N | Sensitive | Data DPD | XGB DPD | Amp. | Best mit. | Inter. max | Single-axis OK? | Intersectional OK? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Stack Overflow 2024 | 65,000 | Age (binary: <35 vs >=35) | 0.305 | 0.567 | 1.86x | 0.001 | 0.977 | NO | NO |
| freeCodeCamp 2018 | 30,047 | Gender (binary: man vs woman) | 0.070 | 0.052 | 0.74x | 0.000 | 0.771 | YES | NO |
| UCI Adult / Census Income | 48,842 | Gender (binary: man vs woman) | 0.195 | 0.096 | 0.49x | 0.007 | 0.171 | YES | NO |

## Interpretation

**Baseline-bias spectrum (low -> high):**
- freeCodeCamp 2018: data DPD = 0.070
- UCI Adult / Census Income: data DPD = 0.195
- Stack Overflow 2024: data DPD = 0.305

**Amplifies the data gap:** Stack Overflow 2024
**Compresses the data gap:** freeCodeCamp 2018, UCI Adult / Census Income

**Intersectional non-compliance:** Stack Overflow 2024, freeCodeCamp 2018, UCI Adult / Census Income cross the |DPD| <= 0.1 line once a second axis (experience) is added. This is the project's headline result -- single-axis compliance can hide order-of-magnitude subgroup disparity.
